data.title: Anthropic reaches deal with music publishers in copyright lawsuit over AI models - report
data.description:
Anthropic has reached an agreement to settle parts of a copyright infringement lawsuit ​​brought by music publishers over alleged training of the company's AI models on protected song lyrics, The Verge reported.

The agreement, which was signed by U.S. District Judge Eumi, requires Anthropic to apply existing guardrails in the training of future AI models and to set up a procedure for music publishers to intervene when copyright infringement is suspected, the report added, citing a court document.

In October 2023, music publishers including Universal Music Group (OTCPK:UMGNF) (OTCPK:UNVGY) and Concord Music filed a copyright infringement lawsuit against Anthropic for allegedly training its AI model on lyrics from at least 500 protected songs, the report noted.

Anthropic, Universal Music Group, and Concord Music have not yet responded to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The music publishers acknowledged that platforms such as Genius already distribute lyrics online, however noted that these websites pay a licensing fee to use the copyrighted songs. Anthropic “intentionally removed or altered copyright management information” from the impacted songs while ingesting data found on those websites to train its AI models, the report added.

Under the agreement, Anthropic noted that it would maintain its already implemented guardrails in its current AI models and products, which intend to prevent its AI models from infringing on copyrighted content. The company will also apply its existing guardrails to any future AI systems it develops, according to the court filing.

At any time during the pendency of the proceeding, the music publishers can notify Anthropic if the guardrails are not effectively preventing the output that reproduces the music lyrics controlled by the publishers. Anthropic would need to respond to the publishers and undertake an investigation into the allegations, and the publishers will cooperate in good faith, as per the court filing.

Claude "isn't designed to be used for copyright infringement, and we have numerous processes in place designed to prevent such infringement,"” Anthropic said in a statement to The Hollywood Reporter.

"We continue to look forward to showing that, consistent with existing copyright law, using potentially copyrighted material in the training of generative AI models is a quintessential fair use," added Anthropic — which is backed by Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Alphabet's (GOOGL) (GOOG) Google — the developer of AI model Claude.

The music publishers behind the original complaint have asked for a preliminary injunction to restrict Anthropic from training future AI models on their protected song lyrics. The court is expected to decide in the coming months, the report added.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures rise after choppy start to 2025
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday, a day after Wall Street kicked off 2025 with a choppy first trading session.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.5% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.55%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.24%.

U.S. stocks on Thursday experienced a choppy first trading session of 2025, in which they initially jumped, then seesawed and finally ended in negative territory. Wall Street is now on a five-day losing streak.

"The first trading day has been a very poor guide to the rest of the year in recent times, so we shouldn’t extrapolate things too far," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

For U.S. equities there was a much more negative story, with the S&P 500 posting a fifth consecutive loss, driven by even deeper losses for the Magnificent 7, Allen added.

On the economic side, the December ISM Manufacturing Index is forecasted to land during market hours. The economists on an average expect it to come in at 48.2.

"A U.S. business sentiment survey is due. Partisan bias gives upside risks," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

There are a couple of Federal Reserve speakers also lined up for today, including Richmond Fed President, Thomas Barkin and San Francisco Fed President, Mary Daly.

data.title: S&P 500's returns on first trading day of a year poor barometer for annual performance
data.description:
Wall Street suffered a choppy first trading session of 2025 on Thursday, eventually ending in the red. But investors need not fret, as history shows that the benchmark S&P 500's (SP500) performance on the first trading day of a year is a poor benchmark for the gauge's eventual annual return.

In this century, the S&P's (SP500) returns on the first trading day of a year has matched its eventual annual performance in 13 instances, while differing in 12.

See the chart below:

Notably, the last four years in a row have seen a discrepancy in the S&P's (SP500) performance on the first trading day versus its eventual annual return.

Market participants are still anticipating another positive year for equities in 2025, though after delivering back-to-back years of +20% gains for the first time since 1998, some of the momentum is expected to ease up. There remains uncertainty over incoming President Donald Trump's policies and the future path of monetary policy.

"Does this week’s stock market behavior provide clues for the rest of the year? Probably not," David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation, said.

"It’s fair to assume that much of the recent volatility and downside pressure comes as a result of year-end window dressing and fund rebalancing, as managers shift their holdings between equities and bonds. This became necessary due to weakness in the bond market since mid-September (ironically just after the Fed slashed rates by 50 basis points) contrasting with strength across equities going back to October 2023," Morrison added.

For investors looking to track Wall Street's three major averages - the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500), the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND), and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) - here are some exchange-traded funds of interest: (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QLD), (TQQQ), (QID), (SQQQ), (DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), and (SDOW).

data.title: Apple Fitness+ unveils new lineup, collaboration with Strava
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) Friday unveiled new features for its Fitness+ streaming workout service, offering new programs for strength, pickleball, yoga, and breath meditation, and a new collaboration with Strava.

The new content will be available starting Monday, January 6, on iPhone, iPad, and Apple TV, the company said in a statement.

Fitness+ is also collaborating with Strava to bring a new integration to the Strava app experience, along with a special Fitness+ trial offer for Strava subscribers, and celebrated athletes from the Strava community will appear as guests in workouts.

“Fitness+ is designed to support everyone’s unique fitness and wellness journey, providing welcoming and motivating workouts, meditations, and programs that make it easy to stay active and mindful,” said Jay Blahnik, Apple’s (AAPL) vice president of Fitness Technologies.

Fitness+ now also offers a strength, endurance, and agility for pickleball program featuring professional pickleball champion Catherine Parenteau, who has been ranked No. 1 in the world in both singles and doubles.

Meanwhile, the artist spotlight series, which dedicates entire workout playlists to a single artist, returns with new workouts featuring music from some of the world’s biggest artists. Upcoming featured artists include Janet Jackson (starting January 13), Coldplay (starting January 20), Bruno Mars (starting January 27), and Apple Music Super Bowl LIX Halftime Show headliner Kendrick Lamar (starting February 3).

Additionally, to kick off the new year, Apple (AAPL) Watch users can earn a limited-edition award when they close all three activity rings for seven days in a row in January.

data.title: Boeing adding more surprise quality checks, as FAA to continue enhanced oversight
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) said Friday it is conducting more surprise inspections at its factories, as part of a broader plan to confront a manufacturing quality crisis that has forced the company to slow production and raised scrutiny from federal regulators.

Among the new procedures instituted by Boeing (BA) are another layer of random quality checks where plane parts are commonly removed and then put back; in the case of last January's jet panel blowout on an Alaska Air flight, workers failed to replace bolts needed to hold a door plug in place after the plug had been opened to repair faulty rivets.

Other measures include inspecting fuselages made by supplier Spirit AeroSystems (SPR) before they leave Spirit's factory, additional worker training, confidentiality safeguards for employees who report problems, and simplified instructions for building 737s.

Federal Aviation Administration head Mike Whitaker said in an online post Friday that the agency has more work to do and will continue to closely monitor Boeing (BA).

Boeing (BA) is making progress, "but this is not a one-year project," Whitaker said. "What's needed is a fundamental cultural shift at Boeing that's oriented around safety and quality above profits. That will require sustained effort and commitment from Boeing, and unwavering scrutiny on our part."

Whitaker is stepping down early from his five-year term on January 20 when President-elect Trump takes office.

data.title: The Surgeon General pushes for cancer warnings labels for alcoholic drinks
data.description:
Alcohol usage was cited by the U.S. Surgeon General as the third leading preventable cause of cancer in the U.S. after tobacco and obesity. The Surgeon General's advisory report indicated that alcohol contributes to 100,000 cancer cases and 20,000 cancer deaths each year. The report also found that alcohol-related cancer deaths shorten the lives of those who die by an average of 15 years.

The report said breast cancer marked the highest percentage of alcohol-related cancer in the U.S., with an estimated 44,180 cases in 2019, marking 16.4% of the approximately 270,000 total breast cancer cases for women.

The Surgeon General, Dr. Vivek Murthy, highlighted that data does not bear out that the current guidelines for no more than two drinks a day for men and one drink a day for women present no cancer risks.

Currently, labels on bottles and cans of alcoholic beverages have warnings about drinking while pregnant or before driving and operating other machinery, as well as general health risks. Congress would have to mandate any new warning labels for the alcoholic beverage industry.

A broad list of alcohol-related stocks includes Brown-Forman (NYSE:BF.A) (BF.B), Heineken N.V. (OTCQX:HEINY), Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD), Molson Coors (TAP), Boston Beer (SAM), Diageo (NYSE:DEO), MGP Ingredients (MGPI), Ambev S.A. (ABEV), SNDL (SNDL), Vintage Wine Estates (OTC:VWESQ), Fresh Vine Wine (VINE), Compañía Cervecerías Unidas S.A. (CCU), LQR House (YHC), Alto Ingredients (ALTO), Duckhorn Portfolio, Carlsberg A/S (OTCPK:CABGY), and Constellation Brands (STZ).

data.title: Rogers sees service revenue growth below guidance
data.description:
Rogers Communications (NYSE:RCI) shares slipped 2% Friday morning after it revised its service revenue growth expectations for full year 2024, citing weakness in media revenue during the fourth quarter.

The company expects annual consolidated service revenue growth of just over 7% for the year, compared to its previously-issued guidance range of 8% to 10%.

All other guidance items remain unchanged, the company said.

“Media revenue in the fourth quarter was softer than expected affecting our revenue target for the year,” said Tony Staffieri, President and Chief Executive Officer, Rogers. “Overall, we continued with industry-leading execution throughout the year and look forward to sharing the details during our fourth quarter call.”

The company plans to release its fourth quarter financial results and outlook for 2025 on Jan. 30, before markets open.

data.title: Innovent wins second China nod for Nuvation-partnered cancer therapy
data.description:
Innovent Biologics (OTCPK:IVBIY) announced that Chinese regulators have approved its second marketing application for lung cancer therapy, Dovbleron, developed with U.S. biotech Nuvation Bio (NYSE:NUVB).

The Chinese biotech announced late Thursday that China's National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) cleared Innovent's (OTCPK:IVBXF) second New Drug Application for the product, making it a first-line option for adults with ROS1-positive non-small cell lung cancer.

The NMPA previously approved the orally delivered tyrosine kinase inhibitor, also known as taletrectinib adipate, as a late-line option for the patient group in December.

"The two NDAs approval of DOVBLERON is a reflection of its potential best-in-class efficacy to fulfill the urgent need of patients with ROS1-positive NSCLC," said Hui Zhou, Innovent's Senior Vice President, adding that the company recently launched the first batch of the product.

NSCLC represents the most prevalent form of lung cancer globally, and about 2.6% of NSCLC patients are believed to be ROS1-positive, a group lacking more effective and tolerable treatments.

In the U.S., Nuvation Bio (NYSE:NUVB), which partnered with Innovent (OTCPK:IVBIY) for Dovbleron through its 2024 acquisition of AnHeart Therapeutics, has submitted an NDA for the product. The FDA has granted priority review for the NDA, with a target action date of June 23, 2025.

data.title: European markets in red as global uncertainties weigh investor sentiment
data.description:
Update: This article has been updated to reflect market movement as of 6.45am ET.

London (UKX) -0.11% Net mortgage approvals for house purchases in the United Kingdom, which is an indicator of future borrowing, decreased to 65,700 in November 2024, below market expectations.

Net consumer credit in the United Kingdom rose by £0.88 billion in November.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.36% Germany's unemployment rate steady at 6.1% in December.

France (CAC:IND) -0.77%

Poland's annual inflation rate edged up to 4.8% in December.

Switzerland's procure.ch Manufacturing PMI edged down to 48.4 in December.

Industrial production in Latvia fell by 3% year-on-year in November.

The Czech Republic's GDP grew by 0.5% Q/Q in 2024.

The unemployment rate in Hungary rose to 4.7% in September-November 2024.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.23% lower, with mining stocks mostly negative, while financials led gains. Investors seem to be wary of global political uncertainties, including instability in France, German elections, and potential tariffs under US President-elect Donald Trump.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the economic front today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield down 3 basis points to 4.55%.

Germany's 10-year yield up 3 basis points to 2.39%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 4.56%.

data.title: FBI firearm background checks fell to a five-year low in 2024
data.description:
The FBI's National Instant Criminal Background Check System reported on Friday that firearm background checks rose 1.0% year-over-year to 2,757,367 in December to mark a new monthly high for the year after soft demand was seen in the summer months.

The all-time high for firearm background checks was March 2021 when 4,691,738 firearm background checks were done in a single month. The last time the FBI did less than 2 million checks in a month was September 2018.

For the full year, FBI firearm background checks were 28,097,025 to mark the lowest annual level since 2019. Ahead of the U.S. election, some analysts predicted that firearm stocks could see significant share price jumps around the election if one party swept the White House, Senate, and House of Representatives.

Stocks that have reacted in the past to firearm demand developments include Smith & Wesson (NASDAQ:SWBI), Sturm Ruger (NYSE:RGR), Vista Outdoor, Sportsman's Warehouse (SPWH), Ammo (POWW), ammunition manufacturer Olin Corporation (OLN), outdoors retailer American Outdoor Brands (AOUT), hunting gear seller Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS), gunfire locator firm SoundThinking (SSTI), and retailer Academy Sports and Outdoors (ASO).
